content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.2 WARN — release_notes.txt reads "Welcome to the TaskTide public beta! We're still testing the new sync feature, so expect rough edges" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/release_notes.txt:1-2), explicitly framing the App Store build as a beta.
Pierre: Apple explicitly says betas/trials don't belong on the App Store — they must go through TestFlight — so review-facing copy that calls the submitted build a "public beta" with "rough edges" can trigger a 2.2 rejection or reviewer questions about app completeness. Rewrite the release notes to describe the shipped feature confidently (e.g. "This update improves sync reliability") without beta/testing language, and confirm the sync feature is actually finished before submission.
